concerns.text: SC-004 states the number of significant taxa is 'measured against the expected range of a few'. 'A few' is not a measurable threshold. It must be replaced with a specific integer range (e.g., 2-5) or a statistical expectation derived from power analysis to be verifiable.

SC-005 states runtime is 'measured against the -hour limit'. The numeric value is missing (placeholder '-hour'). This makes the success criterion unmeasurable. A specific integer (e.g., 6 hours) must be provided.

US-3 and SC-003 define a success criterion of '>60% accuracy'. While measurable, this is a performance target, not a verification of the system's *functionality*. If the model achieves 59%, the system has technically met the FR (it trained and validated) but failed the SC. This creates a 'pass/fail' ambiguity where the system is 'done' but the research failed. Consider separating 'System successfully trains and reports metrics' (FR) from 'Model achieves >60% accuracy' (Research Outcome) to ensure the FR is always verifiable regardless of scientific result.

The assumption 'The analysis will be performed on a sampled subset... if the The full dataset exceeds RAM' contains a typo ('if the The'). While minor, it affects the clarity of the fallback procedure.
